heaviest_rainfall.status: The storm today is ranked highest for the amount of rainfall recorded during one storm.  39.03mm fell at a maximum rate of 2.2mm per hour over 116.3 hours.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 27.0mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: Today, one rain storm lasted for 116.3 hours (ranking highest for longest storm from our records).  It accumulated 39.03mm of rain and it fell at 2.2mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: An active storm has been detected in the vicinity.  The last storm experienced was on 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: A significant rainy period is on-going and is ranked 9th for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 8 consecutive days beginning 07 November 2025.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 6 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (186°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 5%) and rainfall (up 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 359 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 446 m), rainfall (down 0.1 mm), feels like temperature (down 2.5°C), and outside temperature (down 2.3°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 358.5m lower than yesterday, 231.9m lower than the past week, 446.2m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 637m to 1658m and averaged 594.9m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, air moisture should feel ok and near calm conditions. A few light raindrops may fall, low clouds are expected, possibly some fog or persistent clouds, and low sunlight due to cloudy or overcast skies. Changeable conditions.
